paper_title: Geological Study of Volcanic Processes in the Panama Canal Area

methods.description: This method involves conducting field surveys and lithofacies analysis to provide systematic constraints on volcanic processes in the Panama Canal area. The surveys were conducted in collaboration with the Panama Canal Authority and included lithological and structural mapping, as well as petrographic observations.

steps.description: Conduct field surveys in the Gaillard Cut and Pacific locks area of the Panama Canal and nearby areas.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct field surveys in specified areas

steps.description: Collaborate with the Panama Canal Authority for lithological and structural mapping.
Perform petrographic observations of thin sections of volcanic and volcaniclastic rocks.

methods.description: This method involves the geochemical analysis of volcanic and volcaniclastic rocks to characterize their stratigraphic extent and relationships. It includes sample preparation, ICP analysis, and comparison with previous data.

steps.description: Crush samples using a steel jaw crusher and mill to a fine powder.
steps.searchable_summary: crush and mill samples to powder

steps.description: Ignite the fine powder in a furnace to remove volatile substances and determine loss on ignition (LOI) values.
Prepare samples for ICP analysis using the lithium metaborate fusion method.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare samples for ICP using fusion

steps.description: Run ICP-OES and ICPMS to obtain major and trace element abundances.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze samples using ICP-OES and ICPMS

steps.description: Compare data with previous ICP-MS trace element analyses to ensure consistency.
